folio.label: 87r
folio.l: For lettre sleetú , so as we clerkes seyn
There haue I taugút hem , to be charitable
And spende here good , there it is resonable
And there I saw oure dame , a where is she
ñonder in the ©erd , I trowe that she be
Seyde this man , and she wyl come a noon
¢ Ey Maister welcome be ©e , be Seynt Ioún
Seide this wyf , how fare ©e hertily
The frere a risetú vp , ful curteisly
And hire embracetú , in his armes narwe
And kist hire swete , and chirketú as a sparwe
Witú hise lippes , dame q¶ he rigút wel
As he that is ©oure seruau~t , euäydel
Thanked be god , æ ©ow ©af soule and lyf
ñet saw I nat this day , so fair a wyf´
In al the chirche , god so saue me
ñe god amende defautes , Sire q¶ she
Algates wolcome be ©e , be my fay
Graunt mäcy dame , this haue I founde alway
But of ©oure greet goodnesse , be ©oure leeue
I wolde preye ©ow , that ©e nat ©ow greeue
I wol witú Thomas , speke a litel throwe
Theise curatours , ben so negligent , and slowe
To gropen tenderly , a conscience
In shrift , in pächynge , is my diligence
And studye , in Petres wordes , and in Poules
I walke and fisshe , mennes soules
To ©elden iúu crist , his ¸pre rent
To sprede his word , is sette al myn entent
¢ Now be ©oure leue , deere Sire q¶ she
Chidetú hi~ wele , for Seynt charite
He is as angry , as a Pissemyre
Thougú that he haue , al that he can desire
Thougú I hi~ wrye a nygút , & make hi~ warme
And ouer hi leye , my legge and eke myn arme
He gronetú lyke oure Boor , litú in oure sty
Other disport of hi~ , rigút noon haue I
I may nat pleese hi~ , in no maner caas
O Thomas ieo vous dye , Thomas Thomas
This maketú the fend , this must ben amended
Ire is a thyngö , that heye god defended
And ther of wol I speke , a word or twoo
Now Maister q¶ the wyf , or that I goo
What wol ©e dyne , I wol go ther a boute
Now dame q¶ he , now ieo vous die saun© doute
Haue I nat of a Capone , but the lyuere
And of ©oure white bred , no but a shyuere
